pain_point_label: Absent Master Data Management (MDM) for Students & Staff

matches.match_rationale: The core MDM problem is name and identifier fragmentation across systems — 'Juan Cruz', 'Juan C. Cruz', 'J. Cruz' stored as separate records. AI-assisted data transformation can normalize name formats, standardize identifier schemas, and map equivalent fields across SIS, LMS, finance, and HR schemas to a canonical golden record structure. This is precisely the ETL-with-AI-mapping use case in the capability definition.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A transformation layer that ingests raw identity records from each system, applies AI-driven field normalization and schema mapping, and outputs a standardized canonical record set ready for deduplication — reducing manual data cleaning effort by 70–90%.

matches.match_rationale: Entity resolution (duplicate detection) on structured identity data — matching name tokens, birth dates, enrollment dates, campus codes — is a classic structured data classification task. A trained model can score record pairs as 'same person / different person / uncertain' with high precision, replacing the manual review process currently used. Multi-site operators have sufficient record volume to train or fine-tune a lightweight model.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A duplicate-detection classifier that ingests feature vectors from cross-system record pairs (name similarity scores, shared attributes, overlapping enrollment windows) and outputs merge/no-merge decisions with confidence scores, routing low-confidence cases to a human review queue.

matches.match_rationale: Fuzzy name matching — identifying that 'Juan Cruz', 'J. Cruz', and 'Juan C. Cruz' are the same person — is fundamentally a semantic similarity problem. Embedding-based search can surface candidate duplicate pairs across the full student and staff population far more accurately than exact-match or Levenshtein distance approaches, especially for Filipino naming conventions with abbreviations, nicknames, and middle name variations.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An identity resolution search layer that embeds every student and staff record, then at ingestion time queries for semantically similar existing records above a match threshold — presenting candidate duplicates to an admin for confirmation before a new ID is created.

matches.match_rationale: Full MDM remediation is a multi-step workflow: extract records from each system, normalize formats, run similarity matching, cluster duplicates, generate merge proposals, route uncertain cases to human reviewers, execute confirmed merges, and update all downstream systems — exactly the kind of multi-system orchestration workflow this capability is designed for. A single-step tool cannot handle the branching logic and error handling required across four or more siloed systems.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An MDM remediation agent that runs on a nightly schedule: pulls identity deltas from SIS/LMS/HR/finance, applies normalization and matching, auto-merges high-confidence duplicates, queues low-confidence cases in a review dashboard, and writes the golden record back to a master identity store that all systems reference.
